svg.title: HP Badge
svg.desc: Health points status badge with reactive fill bar. SNES-inspired arcade theme.

hw:reasoning.hw:intent: Health bar badge for percentage-based metrics. Data-bound via CSS custom property.
hw:reasoning.hw:approach: 140x20 canonical badge size. CSS state machine drives bar fill and color.
hw:reasoning.hw:tradeoffs: CHOSE inset bevel over stroke border to prevent bleed. CHOSE 6px bar height for legibility at small size.

svg.title: LVL Badge
svg.desc: Level indicator badge. Game Boy-inspired green palette.

hw:reasoning.hw:intent: Level/version badge for numeric values. Iconic Game Boy palette.
hw:reasoning.hw:approach: 140x20 canonical size. Inset bevels for authentic 8-bit feel.
hw:reasoning.hw:tradeoffs: CHOSE authentic 4-color Game Boy palette over modernized. CHOSE dark-on-light text for value field contrast.

svg.title: POWER Badge
svg.desc: Power status badge. Arcade gold palette.

hw:reasoning.hw:intent: MAX power state indicator. Arcade gold palette.
hw:reasoning.hw:approach: 140x20 canonical size. Static bevel system matching set consistency.
hw:reasoning.hw:tradeoffs: REJECTED animation—consistency across badge set is more valuable than individual flair. All arcade badges share identical edge treatment.

svg.title: SCORE Badge
svg.desc: High score display badge. Retro purple palette.

hw:reasoning.hw:intent: Score/points badge for cumulative metrics (downloads, stars).
hw:reasoning.hw:approach: 140x20 canonical size. Purple palette evokes arcade leaderboards.
hw:reasoning.hw:tradeoffs: CHOSE static display over animated counter for simplicity. CHOSE monospace alignment for numeric stability.

svg.title: STATUS Badge
svg.desc: Status indicator badge. NES red palette for critical states.

hw:reasoning.hw:intent: Boss/critical status for attention-demanding states (BOSS, LIVE, CRITICAL).
hw:reasoning.hw:approach: 140x20 canonical size. NES red evokes danger and importance.
hw:reasoning.hw:tradeoffs: CHOSE continuous red gradient to maintain urgency. REJECTED two-tone for visual harmony.

hw:reasoning.hw:intent: Maximum clarity diamond-like glass with prismatic edge refraction
hw:reasoning.hw:tradeoffs: Near-white substrate; rainbow rim spectrum; high specular; inverted for dark backgrounds

g.text: arXiv De Stijl Publication Badge arXiv

hw:reasoning.hw:intent: De Stijl neoplastic form with mathematical blue. Blue is De Stijl primary—appropriate for theorem/proof context suggesting ink, precision, rigor.
hw:reasoning.hw:approach: Same bar structure. White key panel (chalk). Deep slate-blue value panel. Cyan accent line for mathematical coolness. QED-style square indicator.
hw:reasoning.hw:tradeoffs: Chose royal blue over Mondrian cobalt for better contrast with white text. Slate blue gradient maintains depth without competing with accent.

hw:reasoning.hw:intent: arXiv preprint variant—open QED box indicates unverified proof (shared but not yet peer reviewed).
hw:reasoning.hw:approach: Same neoplastic structure. Open square indicator semantically encodes preprint status.
hw:reasoning.hw:tradeoffs: Open vs filled box may be subtle distinction—accepted as appropriate for academic audience.

hw:reasoning.hw:intent: Conference venue variant—lavender accent distinguishes from gold award badges.
hw:reasoning.hw:approach: Same neoplastic structure. Lavender accent line for venue context. Circle indicator instead of star—forums vs prizes.
hw:reasoning.hw:tradeoffs: Lavender less prominent than gold—appropriate for venue (prestigious but not prize-winning).

svg.title: Symposium De Stijl: Best Paper Award

hw:reasoning.hw:intent: De Stijl neoplastic form with classical academy palette. Navy and gold suggest scholarly robes, laurel crowns, institutional gravitas.
hw:reasoning.hw:approach: Marble white key panel. Deep navy value panel. Gold accent line and star indicator for awards. Purple undertone in navy suggests classical purple.
hw:reasoning.hw:tradeoffs: Navy extends De Stijl blue toward purple—justified by classical academic context. Gold star indicator reserved for award badges only.

hw:reasoning.hw:intent: De Stijl neoplastic form applied to illuminated manuscript palette. Black bar dividers create Mondrian grid. Gold accent line replaces yellow primary—scholarly gold leaf tradition.
hw:reasoning.hw:approach: Left black bar (4px), vellum key panel, center black bar with gold accent stripe, burgundy value panel with gradient. Geometric status indicator pulses like candlelight on gold leaf.
hw:reasoning.hw:tradeoffs: Gold replaces De Stijl yellow for scholarly context. Burgundy replaces red—maintains primary color energy with manuscript warmth. Vellum off-white for key panel rather than pure white.

hw:reasoning.hw:intent: Citation count variant maintaining Codex De Stijl architecture.
hw:reasoning.hw:approach: Identical neoplastic structure with numeric value content.
hw:reasoning.hw:tradeoffs: Numeric rendering requires adequate spacing—maintained through same panel proportions.

hw:reasoning.hw:intent: De Stijl neoplastic form with terminal green palette. Green phosphor CRT aesthetic suggests cryptographic verification, command line truth revelation.
hw:reasoning.hw:approach: Light terminal green key panel. Pure black value panel. Emerald accent line. Pulsing indicator suggests active verification process.
hw:reasoning.hw:tradeoffs: Black value panel with green text is less readable than other scholar badges—accepted as authentic to terminal aesthetic. Green provides sufficient contrast.

hw:reasoning.hw:intent: Hash integrity variant—truncated cryptographic hash displayed in terminal aesthetic.
hw:reasoning.hw:approach: Same neoplastic structure. Dimmer hash text suggests computed output. Silver accent line for immutable/metallic seal concept.
hw:reasoning.hw:tradeoffs: Hash truncated for space—ellipsis indicates continuation. Silver shifts from pure green palette but adds semantic meaning (sealed/permanent).

hw:reasoning.hw:intent: ORCID identifier variant—same archival architecture with green accent suggesting ORCID brand.
hw:reasoning.hw:approach: Green accent line references ORCID visual identity while maintaining Archive neoplastic structure.
hw:reasoning.hw:tradeoffs: ORCID green competes slightly with sepia warmth—accepted as brand recognition priority.

hw:reasoning.hw:intent: De Stijl neoplastic form with archival sepia palette. Warm browns suggest aged paper, brass catalog hardware, oak drawer fronts—the material culture of preservation.
hw:reasoning.hw:approach: Cream key panel (index card). Sepia-brown value panel. Brass/bronze accent line. Circular indicator suggests catalog drawer handle.
hw:reasoning.hw:tradeoffs: Brown palette extends beyond strict De Stijl primaries—justified by archival context. Maintains neoplastic structure while adapting color to domain.
